summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is alternating current (AC)?

Alternating Current AC current and voltage vary sinusoidally: V = V₀ sin(ωt), I = I₀ sin(ωt). Capacitor — Current leads voltage by 90°. The other options — 'Constant current', 'Current that only flows one way', and 'Direct current' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is impedance?

Impedance Z = √(R² + (X_L − X_C)²) — total opposition to AC. Resonance — When X_L = X_C (ω = 1/√(LC)), impedance is minimum (Z = R) and current is maximum. The other options — 'DC resistance only', 'Capacitive reactance only', and 'Inductive reactance only' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: In a resistor, voltage and current are:

AC Circuit Components Resistor : V and I are in phase. Alternating Current AC current and voltage vary sinusoidally: V = V₀ sin(ωt), I = I₀ sin(ωt). The other options — '90° out of phase', '180° out of phase', and '45° out of phase' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
